summary: Russia launched an Oreshnik missile strike on Bila Tserkva, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage. Ukraine reported increasing civilian injuries from nightly missile attacks on Kyiv and other cities, while Ukrainian forces claimed a successful drone strike on a Russian fuel pump station. Both sides continue intensive missile and UAV operations across multiple fronts.
key_points: Russia employed an Oreshnik missile against Bila Tserkva, hitting a garage cooperative and nearby industrial buildings, with at least 83 civilians injured (Source: @UkraineNow/81417).
Ukrainian President Zelensky reported three Russian missiles striking a water supply facility, burning a market and damaging dozens of residential buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81417).
Ukrainian special forces (SBU) used drones to attack a Russian fuel pump station supplying Moscow airports, igniting an 800 m² fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
Heavy missile barrage over Kyiv resulted in 81 civilian casualties, including three children, and damage to Lukianivska metro station and Square Mall (Source: @UkraineNow/81432).
Ukrainian forces reported a new wave of Russian cruise missiles over Nizhyn district, heading toward Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Multiple Russian UAVs were observed over Chernihiv and Sumy regions, with some heading toward Kyiv and others targeting southern Black Sea coast (Source: @eRadarrua/93473, @kpszsu/62653).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).
Ukrainian intelligence confirmed the Oreshnik strike on Bila Tserkva (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).

sections.executive_summary: Russia's Oreshnik missile strike on Bila Tserkva marked a significant escalation, targeting civilian infrastructure and causing dozens of injuries. Ukraine's night-time missile barrage on Kyiv increased civilian casualties to 81, while Ukrainian drones successfully hit a Russian fuel hub serving Moscow airports. Both sides maintain high‑intensity missile and UAV operations across multiple fronts, sustaining a volatile combat environment.

points: President Zelensky reported three Russian missiles hitting a water supply facility, igniting a market, and damaging dozens of residential buildings, with at least 83 civilians injured nationwide (Source: @UkraineNow/81417).
Kyiv mayor Vitali Klitschko said night attacks killed one civilian and injured 81, including three children; damage included Lukianivska metro station and Square Mall (Source: @UkraineNow/81432).
Ukrainian special forces (SBU) used drones to strike a Russian fuel pump station feeding Sheremetyevo, Domodedovo and Vnukovo airports, causing an 800 m² fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
Ukrainian forces reported a new cruise missile over Nizhyn district, heading toward Kyiv, indicating continued long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Ukrainian intelligence confirmed the Oreshnik missile strike on Bila Tserkva, corroborating official reports (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Ukrainian UAVs continue targeting Russian rear logistics, though specific recent strikes were not detailed in the provided messages.
Russia launched an Oreshnik missile at Bila Tserkva, striking a garage cooperative and nearby industrial structures, with civilian casualties reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81417).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Donetsk regions, indicating intensified air‑ground coordination (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).
Multiple cruise missiles were observed over Nizhyn district, on a trajectory toward Kyiv, suggesting a broad missile salvo (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Russian UAV activity was reported over Chernihiv (near Korop) and Sumy regions, with some heading toward Kyiv and others toward the Black Sea coast (Source: @eRadarrua/93473, @kpszsu/62653).
Russian forces claimed to have hit Ukrainian military command objects with Oreshnik, Iskander, Kinjal and Tsirkon missiles, though exact locations were not disclosed (Source: @UkraineNow/81431).
Russian missile attacks on Kyiv resulted in civilian casualties and infrastructure damage, reflecting a strategy of targeting urban centers.
Ukrainian reports indicated Russian cruise missiles over Nizhyn district, moving toward Kyiv, highlighting pressure on the eastern approach to the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Russian guided aviation bombs were launched on Kharkiv region, suggesting intensified strikes on eastern front logistics (Source: @kpszsu/62631).
Ukrainian forces claimed liberation of settlements Volokhovka and Shesterek in Kharkiv region, advancing 800‑1200 m on 20 sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30390).
Ukrainian UAV activity targeting Russian rear areas in Poltava and Kharkiv was noted, though specific outcomes were not detailed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30390).
Russian UAVs observed near Olishivka heading toward Desna in Chernihiv, indicating pressure on northern‑eastern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/62649).
No new major ground engagements reported beyond these localized advances.
Ukrainian intelligence reported Russian UAVs over Chernihiv region near Korop, potentially heading toward Kyiv (Source: @eRadarrua/93473).
Russian cruise missiles were observed over Sumy region, with three Shahed drones heading toward Lublin district of Poltava (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67196).
Ukrainian forces noted Russian UAVs near Oleshivka moving toward Desna, indicating continued aerial pressure in the north (Source: @kpszsu/62649).
No confirmed ground engagements on the northern front in the latest messages.
Russian air activity includes cruise missiles over Nizhyn district, a northern approach to Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Ukrainian side reported no significant casualties or territorial changes in the north during this period.
Ukrainian reports of Russian UAVs from the Black Sea towards the southern settlement of Pivdenne, indicating maritime‑to‑land strike paths (Source: @kpszsu/62653).
Russian guided aviation bombs were used in Donetsk region, suggesting continued southern front bombardment (Source: @kpszsu/62624).
Ukrainian side reported no major ground offensives in the south, but civilian casualties continued from missile strikes elsewhere.
Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian rear logistics in Zaporizhzhia region were noted, affecting fuel supplies (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30390).
No new large‑scale territorial changes reported on the southern front.
Russian missile activity remained focused on central Ukraine rather than the southern axis during this window.
Russia deployed over 50 rockets and up to 700 UAVs in a massive strike on Kyiv, according to a monitoring source (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67208).
Ukrainian UAVs successfully struck a Russian fuel pump station, causing a large fire and disrupting fuel supply to Moscow airports (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
Multiple Russian cruise missiles were observed over Nizhyn district heading toward Kyiv, indicating sustained long‑range air attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Russian tactical aviation used guided aviation bombs on Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Donetsk, showing integrated air‑ground operations (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).
Ukrainian air defenses reported intercepting missiles over Kyiv, though civilian casualties still rose to 81 (Source: @UkraineNow/81432).
Ukrainian side claimed ongoing UAV activity over Chernihiv and Sumy, with some drones heading toward Kyiv (Source: @eRadarrua/93473, @mon1tor_ua/67196).

key_developments: Russia's Oreshnik missile strike on Bila Tserkva hit a garage cooperative and nearby facilities, injuring at least 83 civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81417).
Ukrainian President Zelensky reported three Russian missiles striking a water supply plant, burning a market and damaging dozens of homes (Source: @UkraineNow/81417).
Ukrainian special forces used drones to attack a Russian fuel pump station serving Moscow airports, igniting an 800 m² fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
Nighttime missile attacks on Kyiv increased civilian casualties to 81, damaging Lukianivska metro station and Square Mall (Source: @UkraineNow/81432).
Russian cruise missiles were observed over Nizhyn district, on a trajectory toward Kyiv, indicating a large‑scale missile barrage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Russian guided aviation bombs were deployed on Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, showing intensified air‑ground strikes (Source: @kpszsu/62631, @kpszsu/62624).
Ukrainian UAVs successfully struck a Russian fuel hub, disrupting fuel supplies to major Moscow airports (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are escalating long‑range missile strikes, employing the Oreshnik system and cruise missiles to target civilian infrastructure in central Ukraine, raising the risk of higher civilian casualties and potential escalation toward strategic assets. Ukrainian air defenses remain strained, though successful drone attacks on Russian logistics demonstrate Kyiv's growing strike capability. Both sides continue to employ UAVs and guided bombs, sustaining a high‑intensity, multi‑domain conflict across all fronts.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are increasing diplomatic pressure, with EU officials condemning the use of Oreshnik missiles as a terror tactic and calling for stronger sanctions (Source: @UkraineNow/81430). Russia maintains its offensive posture, citing no restraint on missile use, while Ukraine seeks to leverage drone strikes to degrade Russian rear logistics. The conflict remains a focal point of NATO concern, prompting continued military aid and intelligence sharing.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring indicates a surge in Russian UAV and cruise missile activity over northern approaches to Kyiv, suggesting preparation for larger coordinated strikes. Ukrainian drone operations against Russian fuel infrastructure highlight a shift toward targeting logistical nodes deep within Russian territory. Continued civilian casualties in Kyiv and Bila Tserkva underscore the need for enhanced air defense and civil protection measures.
